parameters.description:
Use this tool to reply to an existing Gmail email thread and attach one or more PDF documents stored in Google Drive.

The workflow performs the following steps automatically:

1. Searches Google Drive for each requested PDF filename.
2. Downloads every matching PDF.
3. Attaches the requested document(s) to the email.
4. Appends a standard HTML email signature.
5. Replies to the specified Gmail thread with the attachment(s).

## Required Inputs

### message_id (string)

The Gmail Message ID of the email being replied to.

### body (string)

The email body only.

Do NOT include the email signature.

The workflow automatically appends a standard HTML email signature.

### attachment_filenames (string)

The exact filename of the PDF document stored in Google Drive.

Examples:

Single document

```text
Product_Brochure.pdf
```

Another document

```text
Technical_Datasheet.pdf
```

## Optional Inputs

### to (string)

Recipient email address.

Ignored when replying to an existing Gmail thread but may still be supplied.

### subject (string)

Subject line.

Ignored when replying because Gmail preserves the original thread subject.

## Filename Rules

* Use the exact filename stored in Google Drive.
* Include the file extension (.pdf).
* Filenames are case-sensitive where applicable.
* Each filename should refer to a PDF document.

## Example 1

message_id:

```text
19ed59c0d4798a3a
```

body:

```text
Please find the requested technical document attached.
```

attachment_filenames:

```text
Product_Brochure.pdf
```

## Example 2

message_id:

```text
19ed59c0d4798a3a
```

body:

```text
Please find the requested document attached for your reference.
```

attachment_filenames:

```text
Technical_Datasheet.pdf
```

Do not ask follow-up questions if all required inputs are available.

Return an error only if the specified filename cannot be found in Google Drive.

value.subject: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('subject', `The subject line of the email.`, 'string') }}
value.body: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('body', `The HTML or plain text body of the email.`, 'string') }}
value.message_id: ={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('message_id', `The Gmail Message ID of the email to reply to. Leave empty only when sending a new email.`, 'string') }}
value.attachment_filenames:
={{ /*n8n-auto-generated-fromAI-override*/ $fromAI('attachment_filenames', `A comma-separated list of PDF filenames exactly as they appear in Google Drive.

Examples:
test.pdf

or

brochure.pdf,tds.pdf`, 'string') }}
